---
title: "Chapter 1: Introduction to Solid Rocket Motors and MDO"
author: "Troy Altus"
date: last-modified
---

# Introduction to Solid Rocket Motors and MDO

Solid rocket motors (SRMs) power launch vehicles, missiles, and boosters worldwide due to their **simplicity**, **high reliability**, and **excellent thrust-to-weight ratio**.

## Why Multidisciplinary Design Optimization (MDO)?

Designing an SRM is inherently multidisciplinary. Key disciplines interact strongly:

- **Internal ballistics** — pressure-time curve, thrust profile, burn time
- **Structural analysis** — case burst margin, grain structural integrity under pressure and acceleration
- **Thermal protection & ablation** — insulator sizing, nozzle throat erosion
- **Trajectory & vehicle performance** — total impulse, mass fraction, delivered velocity
- **Manufacturing & cost** — producibility, material usage, complexity

Traditional single-discipline optimization often produces designs that fail when all constraints are considered together. **MDO** provides systematic methods to handle these couplings and find better overall solutions.

This book presents the theory, practical models, coupling strategies, and **executable Python code** (built on NumPy, SciPy, and optionally OpenMDAO) needed to perform full MDO studies on solid rocket motors.

**Book Structure**
- Chapter 2: SRM Fundamentals and Parametric Modeling
- Chapter 3: Propulsion and Internal Ballistics
- Subsequent chapters cover structures, thermal, trajectory, single- and multi-discipline optimization, surrogates, uncertainty, and case studies.
